summary: Conduction aphasia results from dysfunction involving both the arcuate fasciculus and associated gray matter structures such as the superior temporal gyrus and inferior parietal lobule, rather than isolated white matter injury alone. This reflects updated neuropathology emphasizing integrated cortico-cortical circuits essential for phonologic repetition despite preserved language comprehension.
